engine.ladder_reasoning: Visitor is deciding whether to visit a physical venue — the menu, prices, hours, happy-hour times, address and events ARE the decision; browsing photos/menu is the choice, not evaluating a product or trusting a firm.

goal.one_action: get visitors to come eat/drink at the bar (view menu/hours/address, reserve, sign up for event news)

structure.archetype_note: Info-hub venue site: home aggregates hours/happy-hour/address/contact + teasers; dedicated Menu and Calendar pages; reservations and shop offloaded to external links (Linktree, shop subdomain).

unmapped.observed: THIS MONTH / NEXT MONTH (calendar of events, rendered as images)
unmapped.why_no_fit: A dated event schedule is not a canonical section; closest is a generic content block but it is fundamentally an events listing.

proposed_new.name: event-listings
proposed_new.evidence: Dedicated /calendar page with THIS MONTH / NEXT MONTH event schedule; reservations CTA also redirects off-site (Linktree).

confidence.why: fetch_quality full; venue archetype unambiguous (menu, hours, happy-hour, address, calendar). Only soft spot: primary conversion is diffuse (newsletter vs reservations-redirect vs visit).

gap_findings: Venue's real 'conversion' is a physical visit — no single on-site action captures it; taxonomy forces a proxy (newsletter subscribe).
event-listings (dated calendar) and external Linktree reservations redirect are not expressible in the closed vocab.
The FOOD/BEVERAGE menu is a priced menu-list, not truly 'pricing' — canonical sections lack a menu concept, so it maps awkwardly to pricing.
